nodes.label: Bridge Credential Sharing via sessionStorage (bridge_user_id, bridge_api_key)
nodes.rationale: Two independent client modules (groqClient + whatsappClient) share credentials through sessionStorage rather than a shared module. This avoids circular dependencies but creates an implicit contract on storage keys between loosely coupled modules.
nodes.norm_label: bridge credential sharing via sessionstorage (bridge_user_id, bridge_api_key)

nodes.label: Exponential Backoff with Jitter Retry Pattern
nodes.rationale: Both groqClient and whatsappClient independently implement the same retry strategy (getRetryDelay, sleep, isRetryable, MA___RETRIES, RETRYABLE_STATUS_CODES, AbortError handling). This code duplication exists because the modules are intentionally decoupled with no shared utility layer.
nodes.norm_label: exponential backoff with jitter retry pattern

nodes.label: Dynamic CSS Custom Properties Theme Engine (royal, emerald, rose, ocean presets)
nodes.rationale: Full theme definitions are inlined as JS objects in CalendarApp rather than CSS files, applied via document.documentElement.style.setProperty(). This enables runtime theme switching by user preference stored in useSettingsStore without CSS file swapping.
nodes.norm_label: dynamic css custom properties theme engine (royal, emerald, rose, ocean presets)

nodes.label: Supabase Realtime Subscription Initialize/Cleanup Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: CalendarApp useEffect initializes all Supabase stores on user login (events, settings, chat) then sets up real-time subscriptions. The cleanup function unsubscribes all three channels. This centralized init pattern ensures subscriptions are torn down on logout/user-switch to prevent stale listeners.

nodes.label: Offline-First Sync Queue (pendingSync + isOnline in useEventStore)
nodes.rationale: useEventStore maintains a pendingSync queue for changes made while offline. When connectivity is restored (window online event), syncPendingChanges is called. The OfflineIndicator surfaces this queue state to users, and the service worker bypasses API/Supabase requests to avoid stale cache responses for real-time data.
nodes.norm_label: offline-first sync queue (pendingsync + isonline in useeventstore)

nodes.label: Lazy Bridge Registration on Auth - Bridge is optional, calendar works without WhatsApp
nodes.norm_label: lazy bridge registration on auth - bridge is optional, calendar works without whatsapp

nodes.label: Environment Configuration Validator
nodes.rationale: Auth is always required in production; dev mode uses VITE_REQUIRE_AUTH flag to bypass. Env vars baked into Vite client bundle at build time.

nodes.label: isAuthRequired()
nodes.rationale: Auth always enforced in production; dev-only bypass via VITE_REQUIRE_AUTH='false'.

nodes.label: Holiday API Client (Nager.Date)
nodes.rationale: Uses free Nager.Date API (no API key required) with in-memory Map-based caching per year/country.

nodes.label: detectCountryCode()
nodes.rationale: Auto-detects country from browser locale (navigator.language) with timezone-based fallback map; defaults to 'IN'.

nodes.label: Holiday Cache (Map)
nodes.rationale: In-memory Map keyed by year-countryCode to avoid repeated API calls.

nodes.label: Nager.Date API (v3)
nodes.rationale: Free public holiday data API, no API key required.

nodes.label: Device Performance Detection
nodes.rationale: Detects device capabilities (CPU cores, memory, connection, WebGL) to recommend optimal blur level for glassmorphism effects. Respects prefers-reduced-motion accessibility setting.

nodes.label: calculatePerformanceScore()
nodes.rationale: Scores 0-100 weighted: CPU cores (max 30), device memory (max 25), connection type (max 15), pixel ratio (max 10), WebGL (10), mobile penalty (-10). Forces 0 if prefers-reduced-motion is set, for accessibility compliance.

nodes.label: getSupabaseClient()
nodes.rationale: Singleton Supabase client with auto-refresh tokens, localStorage session persistence (key: 'ai-calendar-auth'), and realtime config (eventsPerSecond: 10). Returns null in dev without auth.

nodes.label: sanitizeString()
nodes.rationale: Removes HTML tags, javascript: URLs, and inline event handlers (on*=) for XSS prevention. Optional trim parameter for while-typing scenarios.

nodes.label: useChatStore (Zustand)
nodes.rationale: Optimistic UI: creates temp IDs, syncs to Supabase, then replaces temp ID with DB UUID. Attachments NOT persisted to DB (too large for base64), only shown in current session. Maps 'assistant' (DB) â†” 'ai' (UI) role names.

nodes.label: useEventStore (Zustand)
nodes.rationale: Offline-first with optimistic updates and rollback. Temp ID pattern with Supabase UUID replacement. Field mapping: recurrence_end (DB snake_case) â†” recurrenceEnd (app camelCase). pendingSync queue for offline changes.

nodes.label: useSettingsStore (Zustand)
nodes.rationale: Settings persisted to Supabase profiles table. Blur level system: deviceBlurLevel (auto-detected), blurEnabled (user toggle), blurOverride (manual override). getEffectiveBlurLevel() resolves priority: disabled â†’ override â†’ auto-detect.

nodes.label: handle_new_user() Trigger
nodes.rationale: Auto-creates profiles row on auth.users INSERT, copying email and full_name from user metadata.

nodes.label: get_or_create_bridge_api_key() Function
nodes.rationale: SECURITY DEFINER function callable from frontend to retrieve or generate a WhatsApp bridge API key for the authenticated user.

nodes.label: Row Level Security Policies
nodes.rationale: All tables enforce auth.uid() = user_id (or id) for SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE. Enables multi-tenant isolation at database level.

nodes.label: VITE Environment Variables
nodes.rationale: Baked into client bundle at build time by Vite. Includes: VITE_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, VITE_REQUIRE_AUTH, VITE_BRIDGE_URL, VITE_POLL_INTERVAL, VITE_GROQ_API_KEY.
